page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.7xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with an x86_64 architecture running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile characterized by moderate multi-threaded capabilities contrasted with weaker single-core execution. In single-core tests, the instance scores in the bottom 25% for stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench, with single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests, consistently rank in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register average performance, though the Passmark memory score falls into the bottom 25%.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads that can leverage parallel processing without requiring high memory density. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and moderate database operations, as evidenced by its average performance in Clang compilation and Redis SET operations. However, it is less suitable for single-threaded applications or memory-intensive databases due to its weaker single-core performance and lower memory-per-core allocation.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.7xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of memory. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench, while multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth remains average, though overall memory scores are lower. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.7xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, offering balanced multi-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.7xlarge features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core performance but weak single-core execution
Suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded tasks
